question: Use backtracking to find a subset, if it exists, of the set {2𝈒, 24, 19, 14, 11, 8} with sum a) 41. b) 60.
response: "It looks like the first element is meant to be **20**, so I’ll work with the set:[{20, 24, 19, 14, 11, 8}.]We’ll check subsets systematically (as backtracking would) to hit the target sums.---## a) Sum = 41Try combinations:* (19 + 14 + 8 = 41)✅ This works.**Subset:** ({19, 14, 8})---## b) Sum = 60Check all reasonable combinations:* (24 + 19 + 14 = 57) (close, but short)* (24 + 19 + 11 + 8 = 62) (too big)* (24 + 14 + 11 + 8 = 57)* (20 + 19 + 14 + 8 = 61)* (20 + 24 + 14 = 58)* (20 + 24 + 11 + 8 = 63)Trying all combinations shows none sum to exactly 60.❌ **No subset exists with sum 60.**---## Final Answers* **a)** ({19, 14, 8})* **b)** No solution exists---If you want, I can show the full backtracking tree so you can see exactly how the search explores possibilities."
